name: Cheddar-Crusted Tomato and Roasted Pepper Flan (Weir)
recipeIngredient: Unsalted butter, cut into a dozen pieces and frozen
Roasted red bell peppers, peeled and diced
recipeInstructions:
Prepare the tomatoes by peeling, seeding, chopping, and draining fresh red ripe tomatoes. Canned tomatoes may be substituted.
For the pastry: Pulse flour, butter, and cheddar together in a food processor until the mixture resembles coarse meal. Add shortening and pulse until pieces are slightly larger than butter pieces. Transfer to a bowl. Mix salt and ice water together and add just enough liquid to the flour mixture so it comes together, stirring with a fork. Wrap dough in plastic and let rest in the refrigerator for at least 30 minutes or overnight.
Roll out pastry to a 12-inch circle on a floured surface. Place in a 9-inch pie plate. Crimp edges and prick bottom of pastry. Freeze for 30 minutes.
Preheat oven to 400°F. Line pastry with parchment paper or aluminum foil. Fill with dried beans or metal pie weights and bake until crust edges are golden brown, 15-20 minutes. Remove weights and lining. Return to oven and bake until light golden, 5-7 minutes.
For filling: In a saucepan, bring tomatoes to a boil over high heat. Reduce heat to low and simmer until tomatoes break down and reduce to about half cup, 15-20 minutes. Season with salt and pepper. Let cool.
Whisk eggs, egg yolk, and half-and-half in a bowl. Add tomato sauce and bell peppers. Season with salt and pepper. Pour into pastry shell and bake until set, 30-40 minutes. Cut into wedges to serve.
